standard_variable.semantic_intent: non-CRRT renal replacement therapy active state
standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per positive state episode

approved_boundary_notice.reason: Amsterdam processitems exposes explicit start/stop intervals for itemid 16363 / Hemodialyse, which is narrow enough for non-CRRT RRT active support when CVVH, dialysis-line access, peritoneal catheter, plasma exchange, orders, device parameters, and fluid-removal measurements remain outside the same-name output. Amsterdam opening evidence does not prove peritoneal dialysis active intervals.
approved_boundary_notice.accepted_policy_scope: conservative hemodialysis positive episode boundary for non-CRRT active support

source_filter_lock.required_interval_rule: start, stop, and duration are non-null and positive; stop must be greater than start

database_mapping.source_grain: one Amsterdam processitems Hemodialyse interval row before non-CRRT interval union
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained positive non-CRRT support state episode row after merging overlapping approved Hemodialyse intervals within the stay

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: patientid joined from Amsterdam admissions_core by admissionid
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: not separately available in the current Amsterdam opening mapping
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: raw admissionid is the Amsterdam ICU/MC local admission identifier and normalizes to stay_id under ICU/MC semantics

excluded_statuses: 16352 Peritoneaal catheter peritoneal access catheter, not active dialysis interval
numericitems 8808 Peritoneaaldialyse volume event, not governed active interval
numericitems Hemodialyse onttrekken and CVVH fluid-removal measurements
adjacent_status_boundary.boundary_reason: This same-name variable represents Amsterdam Hemodialyse-derived non-CRRT active support under current source coverage, not umbrella any-RRT, CVVH/CRRT, exact modality truth, access-line presence, peritoneal catheter presence, peritoneal dialysis volume events, fluid-removal events, or renal phenotypes.

approved_source_audit_summary.source_coverage_caution: Amsterdam opening processitems evidence supports Hemodialyse active intervals. It does not prove peritoneal dialysis active intervals; peritoneal catheter and Peritoneaaldialyse numeric volume rows remain adjacent evidence.

state_episode_build_translation.positive_state_translation: retain Amsterdam processitems intervals for itemid 16363 Hemodialyse and merge overlapping or exactly contiguous retained Hemodialyse intervals within the same local admission
state_episode_build_translation.episode_start_translation: use merged source start as Amsterdam relative support_starttime in milliseconds while also emitting relative_start_time_value in minutes
state_episode_build_translation.episode_end_translation: use merged source stop as Amsterdam relative support_endtime in milliseconds while also emitting relative_end_time_value in minutes
state_episode_build_translation.continuity_translation: merge overlapping or exactly contiguous approved Hemodialyse processitems intervals; any positive gap starts a new support episode
state_episode_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained row means no positive non-CRRT RRT episode under this processitems source rule

validation_contract.positive_only_no_row_policy_check: false rows are not emitted; absence of row is not universal proof of no non-CRRT RRT support or no RRT
validation_contract.source_code_lock_rule: all retained rows must originate from Amsterdam processitems itemid 16363 Hemodialyse
notes: This Amsterdam mapping is reviewed-approved for std_non_crrt_rrt_active under current hemodialysis interval source coverage.
The approved source scope is processitems active intervals for Hemodialyse.
Peritoneal active dialysis is not proven by the current Amsterdam opening processitems evidence.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: Formal Amsterdam same-name output is approved only through the governed execution.py path reading this approved mapping spec and producing runtime plus rerun evidence.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading an approved mapping spec and producing runtime plus rerun evidence.
